project.name: heart-beat
project.description: Flutter + Rust app that pairs with a BLE heart-rate strap. This plan adds an always-on background heart-rate monitor: continuous tracking while the app is backgrounded, persistent recording of samples, configurable low-HR alerts, rolling averages, and a Health screen so the user can stay on top of their cardiovascular baseline.

master.title: Background heart-rate monitoring & low-HR alerts
master.goal: Let the user wear the strap all day and trust the app to (a) keep recording HR while backgrounded, (b) notify them when HR has been below a configurable threshold (default 70 bpm) for a sustained window, and (c) surface average / trend stats so they can act on their cardiovascular health.
problems: Today, HR sampling stops or becomes unreliable when the app is backgrounded for long periods on iOS/Android.
There is no persistent time-series store of HR samples — once the session ends, the data is gone, so rolling averages over 1h/24h/7d cannot be computed.
The coaching rule engine focuses on training cues; it has no general-purpose 'sustained low HR' alert that fires outside an active session.
The user cannot configure low-HR threshold, sustained-window duration, sampling cadence, or quiet hours.
There is no Health/at-a-glance screen showing live BPM + rolling averages + trend.

decisions_locked.name: Reuse existing background plumbing
decisions_locked.decision: Build on the Phase-5 background plumbing already in the repo: Android foreground service via flutter_background_service, iOS bluetooth-central background mode, and the Rust BLE reconnect loop. Do NOT introduce a second background runner.
decisions_locked.rationale: PLAN.md shows Phase 5.1/5.2 are already complete. Adding a parallel background mechanism would fragment battery usage and lifecycle handling.

decisions_locked.name: Persist samples via Rust
decisions_locked.decision: Store HR samples in a rolling on-disk store on the Rust side (append-only daily JSONL or SQLite under app docs dir), exposed to Dart through the existing flutter_rust_bridge api. Retention: 30 days by default.
decisions_locked.rationale: Rust already owns the BLE pipeline and survives Flutter UI being torn down on iOS. Keeping persistence next to the data source avoids isolate-boundary loss.

decisions_locked.name: Reuse the coaching cue notification path
decisions_locked.decision: Deliver the low-HR alert through the existing flutter_local_notifications integration in CoachingCueService (or a slim sibling), not a brand-new notification plugin.
decisions_locked.rationale: CoachingCueService already handles permissions, channels, taps, and quiet-hour suppression — extending it costs less than a parallel system.

decisions_locked.name: Default thresholds
decisions_locked.decision: Default low-HR threshold = 70 bpm, sustained window = 10 minutes, sampling cadence = 1 sample / 5s while connected, quiet hours = 22:00–07:00 local, retention = 30 days.
decisions_locked.rationale: Matches the user's stated example (70 bpm) while picking conservative defaults that won't spam notifications for a transient dip.

phases.title: Persistent HR sample store
phases.goal: Every HR sample emitted by the BLE pipeline is appended to a durable, queryable on-disk store so rolling stats and the alert rule have something to read.
context: Rust currently emits HR notifications through an FFI stream consumed by Dart; samples are not persisted beyond the in-memory session store.
The Rust crate already owns the BLE adapter abstraction in rust/src/adapters and rust/src/ports, so the writer should sit alongside those, not in Dart.

included: Daily-rotated JSONL file under app documents directory (timestamp_ms, bpm, rr_intervals?).
Rust-side query API: samples_in_range(start_ms, end_ms), rolling_avg(window_secs), latest_sample().
FRB-bridged Dart API for the new query functions.
excluded: Cloud sync of samples (out of scope; existing cloud_sync_service is unaffected).
ECG / HRV analytics beyond a basic rolling mean.
Migration of historical data — store starts empty.

tasks.text: Add a `hr_store` module under rust/src that opens an append-only JSONL writer at <app_docs>/hr/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl. Each line: {ts_ms, bpm, rr?}. Flush on every write; rotate at local midnight.

code_refs.description: BLE notification handler currently lives here; tap it as the write source

acceptance_criteria: Killing and relaunching the app preserves prior-day files.
A 24-hour soak produces exactly one file per local day.

tasks.text: Wire the BLE HR notification path so every accepted sample is forwarded to `hr_store::append`. Drop duplicates within 500 ms (some straps spam).

code_refs.description: HR stream wiring; add the store hook here

acceptance_criteria: While the app is backgrounded on Android (foreground service active), JSONL grows continuously.
On iOS, JSONL grows whenever the BLE central is active in background.

tasks.text: Implement Rust query functions: `samples_in_range(start_ms, end_ms) -> Vec<Sample>`, `rolling_avg(window_secs) -> Option<f32>`, `latest_sample() -> Option<Sample>`. Read across files spanning the range.
acceptance_criteria: Unit test in rust/src for boundary cases (empty store, single file, multi-day range).
Returns within 200ms for a 7-day range with ~120k samples.

tasks.text: 30-day retention sweep: on Rust init, delete `<app_docs>/hr/*.jsonl` older than 30 days.
acceptance_criteria: Test that touches files with old mtimes and verifies they are removed.

tasks.text: Regenerate FRB bindings and add a thin Dart wrapper service `lib/src/services/hr_history_service.dart` that exposes `samplesInRange`, `rollingAvg`, `latestSample`.
acceptance_criteria: Dart unit test calls each method on a mocked Rust side and gets typed results.
After a 1-hour background run on a real device, the JSONL file contains > 600 samples and `rolling_avg(3600)` returns a plausible value within 200ms.
App relaunch sees the prior file and is ready to query immediately.

phases.title: User settings for monitoring
phases.goal: Give the user a Settings screen to configure low-HR threshold, sustained-window duration, sampling cadence, quiet hours, and notification opt-in.
context: SharedPreferences is already a dependency. CoachingCueService at lib/src/services/coaching_cue_service.dart shows the existing pattern for persisted user prefs.
Defaults: threshold=70, sustained=10min, cadence=5s, quiet hours=22:00–07:00.

included: New `HealthSettingsService` in lib/src/services with typed getters/setters and a ChangeNotifier-style listenable.
New `health_settings_screen.dart` under lib/src/screens with form widgets.
Wire into existing app navigation (settings/drawer entry).

tasks.text: Create `HealthSettingsService` with keys: `health_low_hr_threshold` (int, default 70), `health_sustained_minutes` (int, default 10), `health_sample_cadence_secs` (int, default 5), `health_quiet_start` / `health_quiet_end` (HH:mm strings, defaults 22:00 / 07:00), `health_notifications_enabled` (bool, default true).
acceptance_criteria: Service round-trips every key through SharedPreferences.
Notifies listeners on change.

tasks.text: Build `HealthSettingsScreen` with form fields: number input for threshold (40–120 clamp), slider/stepper for sustained minutes (1–60), dropdown for cadence (1s/5s/15s/60s), two TimeOfDay pickers for quiet hours, master notifications toggle.
acceptance_criteria: Widget test: change threshold to 65 → service emits update with value 65.
Quiet-hours picker rejects invalid HH:mm and clamps to range.

tasks.text: Add a navigation entry (drawer or settings list) that opens HealthSettingsScreen. Reuse existing app navigation conventions.
acceptance_criteria: From the app home, the user can reach HealthSettingsScreen in <= 2 taps.
All five settings persist across app restart and emit listenable changes.

phases.title: Sustained-low-HR alert rule
phases.goal: Whenever the rolling average HR over the user's sustained-window is below the threshold (and we are NOT in quiet hours), fire exactly one local notification, then debounce until the condition clears.
context: rust/src/coaching already hosts the rule engine for training cues; this rule is health-baseline, not training-specific. We add it as a sibling rule that runs whenever the BLE session is active, regardless of training mode.
Notification delivery on the Dart side is owned by CoachingCueService — we extend it (or add a sibling) rather than introducing a new plugin.

included: Rust rule that consumes hr_store + settings + clock and emits a 'sustained_low_hr' cue.
Debounce: don't re-notify until rolling avg has gone above (threshold + 5 bpm) for at least 5 minutes (hysteresis).
Quiet hours: rule output suppressed when local time is within [quiet_start, quiet_end).
excluded: Per-zone training cues (already covered by Phase 5 of PLAN.md).

tasks.text: Add a `low_hr_rule` to rust/src/coaching that, on each tick (every 30s while connected), computes rolling_avg over `health_sustained_minutes`. If below threshold and not in quiet hours and not currently in 'firing' state, emit cue { type: 'sustained_low_hr', avg_bpm, window_min }. Hysteresis as described in the goal.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests cover: never-below, brief-dip-then-recover (no fire), sustained-dip (one fire), continued-dip (no second fire), recovery-then-redip (one new fire), quiet-hours-suppressed.

tasks.text: Plumb settings (threshold, sustained, quiet hours, notifications-enabled) from `HealthSettingsService` into the Rust rule via FRB. Re-push on change.
acceptance_criteria: Changing the threshold in the UI updates the rule within one tick (≤30s).

tasks.text: On the Dart side, subscribe to the cue stream and, when `sustained_low_hr` arrives AND notifications are enabled, present a `flutter_local_notifications` notification: title = 'Heart rate low', body = 'Average HR was {avg_bpm} bpm over the last {window_min} min'. Tap opens the Health screen.
acceptance_criteria: Manual test on Android: with threshold = 200 (forces fire), one notification appears within 30s.
Notifications stop when master toggle is off.
End-to-end: with threshold 70 and a real strap, walking-pace HR (~85bpm) does NOT fire; resting HR (~65bpm sustained 10 min) fires exactly once.
Quiet hours fully suppress the notification.

phases.title: Health screen (live + averages)
phases.goal: One glanceable screen showing current BPM, rolling averages over 1h / 24h / 7d, a 24h sparkline, and the current rule state ('OK' / 'Below threshold for X min').
context: Existing screens live under lib/src/screens — reuse the app shell.

included: New `health_screen.dart` with live BPM, three average cards, a 24h sparkline.
excluded: Workout detail charts (already covered by session screens).

tasks.text: Build `HealthScreen` widget with three Card tiles (1h / 24h / 7d averages) calling `HrHistoryService.rollingAvg` on init and on pull-to-refresh.
acceptance_criteria: Tiles show '—' when the store is empty, a number with units otherwise.
Pull-to-refresh re-queries and updates within 500ms.

tasks.text: Add a 24h sparkline using fl_chart, downsampled to ~288 points (5-min buckets). Live BPM is overlaid as the rightmost point and updates from the existing HR stream.
acceptance_criteria: Empty store renders a placeholder, not a crash.
On a 1h+ run, the chart shows a non-flat curve.

tasks.text: Add a 'Status' banner at the top of HealthScreen reflecting the rule state: green 'OK', amber 'Below {threshold} bpm for {N} min'. Read from the same cue stream as the notification.
acceptance_criteria: Banner color and text update in real time as the rule transitions.

tasks.text: Wire navigation: a 'Health' entry in the app drawer / bottom nav opens HealthScreen. Notification taps from Phase 3 deep-link here.
acceptance_criteria: Notification tap opens HealthScreen even if the app was cold-started.
User can open the Health screen, see live BPM, 1h / 24h / 7d averages, a sparkline, and the current rule status.

phases.title: Tests, soak, and docs
phases.goal: Lock in the behavior with automated tests and a manual soak; add a short doc so the next contributor can extend the rule.
context: Phase 5.6 of PLAN.md established an 8-hour soak template — we reuse it.

included: Rust unit tests for hr_store and low_hr_rule.
Dart widget tests for HealthSettingsScreen and HealthScreen.
Manual soak instructions appended to the relevant existing doc.
CLAUDE.md quickref entry under 'Common Commands' for any new scripts.
excluded: End-to-end integration tests on real hardware (covered by manual soak).

tasks.text: Add Rust unit tests covering hr_store rotation, retention sweep, and rolling_avg correctness across day boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: `cargo test` passes; new tests cover the boundary cases listed in the goal.

tasks.text: Add Rust unit tests for low_hr_rule covering the six scenarios listed in 3.1's acceptance criteria.
acceptance_criteria: All six scenarios have a named test that asserts the expected fire count.

tasks.text: Add Dart widget tests for HealthSettingsScreen (each field round-trips through the service) and HealthScreen (renders empty state, renders populated state, banner reflects rule state).
acceptance_criteria: `flutter test` passes; new tests are listed by file.

tasks.text: Update PLAN.md and CLAUDE.md: add a milestone row for this work and document any new scripts or how to flip the threshold for manual testing.
acceptance_criteria: PLAN.md shows the new feature and its checkbox state.
CLAUDE.md mentions any added scripts or test entry-points.

tasks.text: Run a 4-hour on-device soak with the rule armed at threshold 70 / sustained 10min. Verify: no missed samples, exactly the expected number of notifications, battery drain < 10% per hour.
acceptance_criteria: Soak log captured under .soak-logs/ with sample count, notification timestamps, and battery delta.
All new tests pass. Manual soak log committed.
